data.title: Amazon to Acquire Globalstar for $11.6 Billion to Boost Satellite Operations
data.description: Amazon has agreed to acquire satellite company Globalstar for $11.6 billion. According to Odaily, this move is expected to strengthen Amazon's efforts to compete with Elon Musk's Starlink project by enabling the development of its own low Earth orbit satellite business.

data.title: SkyMapper L1 Launches on Avalanche for Verifiable Telescope Data
data.description: SkyMapper has introduced SkyMapper L1, a network built on Avalanche, designed to transform telescope observations into cryptographically verifiable digital records. According to NS3.AI, this network ensures that each observation is signed, time-stamped, and anchored with a digital fingerprint on Avalanche. The SETI Institute is actively contributing live observational data to this initiative.

data.title: Ledger Appoints Chief Human Agency Officer to Enhance AI Roadmap
data.description: Ledger has introduced a new role of Chief Human Agency Officer, appointing Ian Rogers, the current Chief Experience Officer, to the position. According to NS3.AI, this move is part of Ledger's strategy to support its AI roadmap. The company plans to implement a human-in-the-loop review feature later this year and a Proof of Human system in the final quarter.

data.title: IMF Chief Economist Warns of Potential Global Recession and Rising Inflation
data.description: The International Monetary Fund's Chief Economist, Gita Gopinath, has issued a warning about the possibility of a severe economic scenario that could lead some economies into a full-scale recession. According to Jin10, Gopinath highlighted concerns that global inflation rates could exceed 6%, making it challenging to control inflation expectations. This warning underscores the potential risks facing the global economy, as inflationary pressures continue to mount.

data.title: Aave Dominates DeFi Market with 30% of Total Value Locked
data.description: Aave has emerged as a significant player in the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector, accounting for 30% of the total value locked (TVL) across the market. According to NS3.AI, this highlights Aave's growing influence and adoption within the DeFi ecosystem. The platform's dominance underscores its role in shaping the future of decentralized financial services.

data.title: The US dollar has regained its safe-haven appeal, and its positive correlation with the VIX fear index is approaching its 2024 peak.
data.description: The Iran war has restored the traditional relationship between the US dollar and stock market volatility: safe-haven investors are flocking back to US assets that were shunned after last year's tariff turmoil. Since the start of the war, the positive correlation between the dollar and the VIX volatility index has continued to strengthen, currently approaching its highest level since 2024, reverting to the pattern of the past five years where "the dollar rises during market volatility and falls during market calm." With the US closing the Strait of Hormuz, Scotiabank believes the relationship between the dollar and the VIX warrants attention, especially given the potential for future stock market volatility to be suppressed. The bank's chief foreign exchange strategist, Sean Oss, stated on Monday, "If the situation in the Gulf fails to trigger a significant and sustained rebound in the VIX, the dollar could extend its decline further." (Jinshi)

data.title: Neo Foundation Restructuring Proposed Amid Governance Deadlock
data.description: Neo founder Da Hongfei has put forward a proposal to restructure the Neo Foundation, citing a governance impasse between the project's two founders. According to NS3.AI, Da Hongfei aims to transform the Neo Foundation into an institution that operates independently of its founders, ensuring more effective stewardship of the project.

data.title: Musk's Vision for a 'Super App' Nears Completion
data.description:
Elon Musk is reportedly close to finalizing his ambitious plan for a 'super app.' According to PANews, the app aims to integrate various services into a single platform, potentially revolutionizing how users interact with technology. Musk's vision includes features such as social networking, e-commerce, and financial services, all accessible through one application.

The concept of a 'super app' is inspired by successful models in Asia, where apps like WeChat have become integral to daily life by offering a wide range of services. Musk's approach seeks to replicate this success in other markets, leveraging his existing ventures and technological expertise.

While details about the app's specific functionalities remain sparse, industry experts speculate that it could incorporate elements from Musk's companies, such as Tesla and SpaceX, to create a unique user experience. The integration of these services could streamline operations and enhance user convenience.

Musk's initiative reflects a broader trend in the tech industry, where companies are increasingly seeking to consolidate services to capture more user engagement and data. As the project progresses, it is expected to face challenges related to privacy, security, and regulatory compliance.

The development of Musk's 'super app' is being closely watched by competitors and analysts alike, as it could set new standards for digital platforms and influence future technological innovations.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Nominee Hearing Scheduled for April 21
data.description: The hearing for the Federal Reserve Chair nominee, Philip N. Wos, is set for April 21, according to Odaily. JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has previously expressed that Wos is an excellent choice for the position, emphasizing that his confirmation should proceed swiftly. Meanwhile, U.S. Senator Rick Scott has voiced confidence that the Department of Justice will soon conclude its investigation into the current Federal Reserve Chair, Jerome Powell.

data.title: Ledger Unveils 2026 AI Security Roadmap to Ensure Human Control
data.description:
Ledger, a manufacturer of cryptocurrency hardware wallets, has released its 2026 AI security roadmap aimed at the AI agent economy, focusing on maintaining human control. According to Odaily, the company has appointed Chief Experience Officer Ian Rogers as the first Chief Human Intelligence Officer to lead the implementation of the AI security strategy.

The roadmap is divided into four phases: an open hardware integration toolkit is already available; in Q2, AI agent hardware-bound identity will be introduced; Q3 will see the launch of agent intentions and strategies, supporting hardware-trusted approvals; and Q4 will add a 'proof of humanity' feature. Ledger emphasizes the use of hardware security roots to provide a final confirmation layer for AI agent transactions, mitigating asset and identity risks, and aiding developers in building secure agent payment infrastructures.

data.title: Analysis: Market expectations are overly focused on crude oil; US March PPI fell short of expectations across the board.
data.description: Investinglive analyst Adam Button stated that the US March overall and core PPI annual and monthly rates were all significantly lower than market expectations. Given that market forecasts primarily revolved around the anticipated surge in energy prices, the focus is on where the discrepancies lie. While energy prices did indeed rise sharply, the increase was less than expected: refined oil products surged (gasoline +15.7%, diesel +42.0%, etc.), but natural gas plummeted by 51.7%, partially offsetting the overall impact. The services sector unexpectedly remained flat (0.0% month-on-month), and this sector, accounting for approximately 68% of the total, was the main reason for the weaker-than-expected data. One driving factor was declining trade margins, with retailers absorbing some energy costs rather than passing them on. Transportation prices rose by 1.3%, but with only 5% of the total, it was insufficient to offset the shortfall. Food prices fell by 0.3%, also dragging down the overall data. In short, market expectations overemphasized crude oil, underestimating three factors: the plunge in natural gas prices, the compression of trade margins, and the slowdown in core services inflation. Energy transmission did exist, but its magnitude was narrow, and the pricing power of other parts of the economy weakened. (Jinshi)

data.title: American Express Expands AI Payment Solutions with New Developer Toolkit
data.description:
American Express has announced an expansion into the AI payment sector with the launch of a developer toolkit aimed at 'agent-based commerce.' According to Foresight News, the toolkit is designed to support AI agents in conducting payments and other economic activities on behalf of users. It includes mechanisms such as payment credential access for verified agents and cardholder identity verification to reduce fraud and dispute risks.

American Express also highlighted its established mechanisms for handling transaction disputes, which can provide protection against errors made by AI agents. This initiative is expected to enhance user trust and increase transaction volumes. Additionally, the company has developed various plans concerning stablecoins, potentially exploring their use in settlement scenarios rather than direct payments, though specific details have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Steady Following Lower-than-Expected PPI Data
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields remained stable after the release of Producer Price Index (PPI) data, which showed a smaller increase than anticipated. According to Jin10, the PPI data suggested that inflationary pressures might be easing, which could influence the Federal Reserve's future monetary policy decisions. The stability in Treasury yields reflects investor sentiment that the Federal Reserve may not need to raise interest rates as aggressively as previously thought. This development comes amid ongoing discussions about the U.S. economic outlook and the potential impact of inflation on financial markets.

data.title: Italian Prime Minister Suspends Defense Pact with Israel Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has suspended a defense agreement with Israel that has been in place for over 20 years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this decision comes in response to the ongoing conflict with Iran, which has resulted in thousands of casualties and a significant increase in oil and gas prices. The suspension reflects Italy's strategic reassessment amid escalating tensions in the region.

data.title: Bank of Japan Governor: Economy and Prices Align with Expectations
data.description: Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda stated that the current state of Japan's economy and prices is largely in line with the central bank's expectations. According to Jin10, Ueda's comments suggest that the Bank of Japan's monetary policy is effectively supporting the country's economic objectives. The alignment of economic indicators with the bank's forecasts indicates stability in Japan's economic environment. Ueda's remarks come amid ongoing assessments of global economic conditions and their potential impact on Japan's financial landscape.

data.title: Federal Reserve Chair Nominee Walsh Clears Confirmation Hurdle
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair nominee Walsh has overcome obstacles to his confirmation, according to Odaily. U.S. Senator Scott announced that Walsh's hearing is scheduled for next week, with expectations that his nomination will be approved.

data.title: Bitfinex report: Tokenized commodities market capitalization surpasses $7 billion, stablecoin infrastructure upgrades accelerate.
data.description: A report released by Bitfinex points out that tokenized commodities are moving from early experiments to practical applications, becoming a significant manifestation of the reconstruction of real-world assets (RWAs) on the blockchain. Its core is not creating new demand, but rather reshaping existing market infrastructure. Data shows that the total market capitalization of tokenized commodities has reached approximately $7 billion, growing by nearly 600% since the beginning of 2025. Current major participants include crypto-native investors and high-net-worth individuals. Against the backdrop of escalating geopolitical volatility, tokenization is improving asset liquidity and risk management flexibility. Gold remains the primary entry point, with Tether Gold holding nearly 40% market share. The report points out that on-chain gold possesses characteristics such as real-time transfer and global auditability, making it more suitable as collateral than physical gold, and can overcome traditional transaction time and settlement limitations. In addition to gold, tokenized commodities have expanded to oil, natural gas, and agricultural products, with soybeans and soybean oil each valued at approximately $400 million, and green finance-related exposure reaching approximately $850 million, demonstrating the potential for cross-category expansion. Meanwhile, blockchain's traceability enhances supply chain transparency and meets regulatory and ESG requirements. Bitfinex believes that future tokenization will shift its focus from precious metals to industrial commodities such as copper and oil, achieving a transformation from "product innovation" to "market infrastructure upgrades" by improving collateral efficiency, asset turnover speed, and transparency.

data.title: EU to Propose Measures to Address Energy Price Impact from Iran Conflict
data.description: The European Union is set to introduce a series of measures next week aimed at mitigating the impact of the Iran conflict on energy prices across the continent. Bloomberg posted on X that these initiatives are also designed to accelerate the electrification of the economy, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and preventing future energy shocks. The EU's strategy comes in response to the ongoing geopolitical tensions, which have significantly affected global energy markets. By focusing on sustainable energy solutions, the EU aims to enhance energy security and stability in the region.

data.title: American Express launches AI-powered payment agent development kit to explore stablecoin settlement application scenarios.
data.description: Payment giant American Express announced an accelerated push into AI payments and launched a developer toolkit for "Agentic Commerce," promising purchase protection against erroneous transactions generated by registered AI agents. This toolkit aims to support AI agents in completing payments and other economic activities on behalf of users, while mitigating fraud and dispute risks through mechanisms such as granting payment credentials only to verified agents and verifying cardholder identity. Furthermore, American Express stated that it already has a mature mechanism for handling transaction disputes, providing protection against AI agent errors, thereby increasing user trust and driving transaction volume growth. It also has "multiple plans" for stablecoins, reportedly exploring stablecoin settlement scenarios rather than payments, but specific details were not disclosed. (Fortune)

data.title: Bitcoin Options Show Decline in Implied Volatility Amid Market Adjustments
data.description:
Bitcoin's price has been on the rise, yet the implied volatility (IV) of major-term options is decreasing. According to Odaily, this trend is accompanied by a noticeable positive skew. The primary reason for these developments is the easing of military tensions between the U.S. and Iran, which has reduced market concerns over war risks, leading to a significant drop in the price of put options.

Trading volumes in both order books and block trades are relatively balanced, with most transactions focusing on the current and following months. The market is readjusting its position layouts, and there is a growing consensus among major participants regarding future market trends. The expectation of low volatility is becoming the mainstream outlook.
